## Identity

## Edges

- DEFEATS: Tully (track_b: Result)

## Origins

When [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), the new [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North), marches west from [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun), he leaves his uncle, Ser [Edmure Tully](wiki:Edmure_Tully), to hold Riverrun and guard his rear.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-8) Edmure commands the [rivermen](wiki:Rivermen), as his father, Lord [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully), is ill.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Robb's [northmen](wiki:Northmen) and [Freys](wiki:House_Frey) bypass the [Golden Tooth](wiki:Golden_Tooth) using a goat track found by [Grey Wind](wiki:Grey_Wind), Robb's [direwolf](wiki:Direwolf).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-7) Near [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport), Robb surprises the newly-raised host of [Stafford Lannister](wiki:Stafford_Lannister), who neglected to post enough sentries, in the [Battle of Oxcross](wiki:Battle_of_Oxcross).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-9) The northmen [capture Ashmark](wiki:Taking_of_Ashemark) and pay the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) back in kind for the devastation inflicted on the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-10)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-8)

Expecting [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) to be preoccupied for months with the [siege of Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)), Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) risks leaving [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal) to stop Robb's raiding of his homeland.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-15) In response, Edmure orders Ser [Helman Tallhart](wiki:Helman_Tallhart) to leave the [Twins](wiki:Twins) and aid Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton), who is at the [crossroads](wiki:Crossroads), with recovering Harrenhal. Edmure thinks that once Robb returns from the west, Tywin can be trapped between Riverrun and Harrenhal and then defeated. Edmure's plan is supported by Lords [Tytos Blackwood](wiki:Tytos_Blackwood) and [Jonos Bracken](wiki:Jonos_Bracken),(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-11) fierce rivals who usually disagree.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1)

In order to return to the westerlands, Tywin must cross the [Red Fork](wiki:Red_Fork) of the [Trident](wiki:Trident). Edmure calls his banners, approximately eight thousand foot and three thousand horse, to prevent Tywin, who has near twice as many, from crossing the river.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-12) While returning to Riverrun, [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark) spots the tents of Lord Blackwood and Ser [Marq Piper](wiki:Marq_Piper), as well as [Darrys](wiki:House_Darry) and [Paeges](wiki:House_Paege).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-13) Edmure allows [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) to seek refuge in Riverun.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-14)

## Aftermath

[Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) simultaneously celebrates Ser [Edmure Tully](wiki:Edmure_Tully)'s victory in the Battle of the Fords and [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark)'s [storming of the Crag](wiki:Storming_of_the_Crag).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) The [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North) is wounded at the Crag, however, and treated by [Jeyne Westerling](wiki:Jeyne_Westerling). After being told that his brothers, [Bran](wiki:Bran_Stark) and [Rickon](wiki:Rickon_Stark), have [been killed](wiki:Capture_of_Winterfell), the grieving Robb sleeps with Jeyne, and then marries her to preserve her honor.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2) Since Robb has broken his betrothal to a daughter of Lord [Walder Frey](wiki:Walder_Frey), the [Freys](wiki:House_Frey) at the Crag and Riverrun angrily depart.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-3) The Freys at Harrenhal are also outraged.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)

After Robb returns to Riverrun, he and Ser [Brynden Tully](wiki:Brynden_Tully) criticize Edmure for having confronted Tywin. They explain that Brynden's scouts had found an advantageous position on the [Goldroad](wiki:Goldroad). Robb had hoped to draw Tywin into the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) and then either defeat Tywin on the Goldroad or lead the westermen on a chase along the [Sunset Sea](wiki:Sunset_Sea)'s coast. Robb explains [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) would have been able to conquer [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-4) Edmure's defense of the fords delayed Tywin long enough for riders from [Bitterbridge](wiki:Bitterbridge) to inform him of happenings in the east, however. With Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) having negotiated an alliance between [House Tyrell](wiki:House_Tyrell) and [House Baratheon of King's Landing](wiki:House_Baratheon_of_King's_Landing),(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) Tywin turned back east, rendezvousing with Lords [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell), [Mathis Rowan](wiki:Mathis_Rowan), and [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly). The combined army then defeated Stannis in the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater).(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Racok62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)

To make amends, Edmure offers to lead Robb's van in the next battle.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-5) After the passing of Lord [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully), Edmure, now Lord of Riverrun, is convinced by Robb, Brynden, and [Catelyn](wiki:Catelyn_Stark) that he should wed one of Walder's daughters, so that the Freys will again support Robb.(wiki:Battle_of_the_Fords.cite_ref-Rasos35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)

>

## Origins

During the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings), King [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) takes [Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)) after the death of its castellan, Ser [Cortnay Penrose](wiki:Cortnay_Penrose).(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racok44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) He appoints Ser [Gilbert Farring](wiki:Gilbert_Farring) as the new castellan, with Lord [Elwood Meadows](wiki:Elwood_Meadows) as Gilbert's second.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Rasosappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) After his defeat in the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater), Stannis sails for the [Wall](wiki:Wall) to aid the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) against [Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder),(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Rasos73.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) leaving Storm's End and [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) lightly held in the south.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Radwd19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

After [the wedding](wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell) of King [Tommen I Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) and [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell) in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), the bride's father, Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell), travels south with half of [House Tyrell](wiki:House_Tyrell)'s strength to [besiege Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(300)).(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Raffc16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) After learning of Margaery's imprisonment by the [Faith](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven), however, Mace abandons his siege and returns to the capital.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Raffc43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) Mace leaves a token force(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Radwd61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) under the leadership of Lord [Mathis Rowan](wiki:Mathis_Rowan) to maintain their siege of Storm's End.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Rawoiafmathis_rowan.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

The [Golden Company](wiki:Golden_Company) sails on [Volantene](wiki:Volantis) ships from [Essos](wiki:Essos) to [Westeros](wiki:Westeros) as part of their plan to crown Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(Young_Griff)), widely thought to have been killed during [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion), in opposition to Tommen.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Radwd61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) At [Griffin's Roost](wiki:Griffin's_Roost), [Haldon Halfmaester](wiki:Haldon) informs Lord [Jon Connington](wiki:Jon_Connington) that Lord Tyrell has returned to King's Landing. Jon tells the captains of the company that capturing Storm's End would give them a secure base and boost Aegon's prestige. When Jon informs Aegon four days later, the prince agrees with the plan but surprises Jon by stating he wants to lead the attack himself.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Radwd61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

The [small council](wiki:Small_council) of King [Tommen I Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) hears reports of the Golden Company marching on the castle with Jon and the pretender Aegon.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Radwdepilogue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

## Narrative Arc

While Princess [Arianne Martell](wiki:Arianne_Martell) travels from [Mistwood](wiki:Mistwood) to Griffin's Roost, she overhears [Chain](wiki:Chain) talking about Storm's End. Once at Griffin's Roost, she is told by Haldon that Storm's End has fallen and that Lord Connington awaits her there.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Arianne_II_1-1)'

## Aftermath

An army from King's Landing, assumed by Ser [Daemon Sand](wiki:Daemon_Sand) to be led by Lord Tyrell, is descending on Storm's End. Haldon claims that Aegon means to meet this army in open battle. Daemon privately advises Arianne against traveling to Storm's End to meet with Jon, but she insists upon going.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Arianne_II_1-2)

## Quotes

A nigh-impregnable stronghold, and [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon)'s last foothold in the south. Once taken, it will give us a secure fastness to which we may retreat at need, and winning it will prove our strength.
>

>
> —[Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell)

> Doubt, division, and mistrust will eat the very ground beneath [your boy king](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon), whilst [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(Young_Griff)) raises his banner above [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) and the [lords](wiki:Lord) of [the realm](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms) gather round him.(wiki:Taking_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Radwdepilogue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2)

>
> —thoughts of [Arianne Martell](wiki:Arianne_Martell)

>
> —[Haldon](wiki:Haldon) to [Arianne Martell](wiki:Arianne_Martell)

## Origins

[Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), [Lord of Storm's End](wiki:Lord_of_Storm's_End), took the majority of the [stormlands](wiki:Stormlands)' forces with him after the [battles at Summerhall](wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall). He left his younger brother, [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), and a small garrison to hold [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End).(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

Following Robert's defeat by Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly) at the [Battle of Ashford](wiki:Battle_of_Ashford),(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Rasos78.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) driving Robert away from his base of power, the Tyrell host defeated resistance in the stormlands and marched on Storm's End. The castle's granaries and storehouses were only half full, as war had been unexpected.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_stormlands:_storm.27s_end.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

## Narrative Arc

Soon after Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell) began besieging Storm's End with the [Reach](wiki:Reach)'s host, Lord [Paxter Redwyne](wiki:Paxter_Redwyne) arrived with the [Redwyne fleet](wiki:Redwyne_fleet) from the [Arbor](wiki:Arbor) and closed [Shipbreaker Bay](wiki:Shipbreaker_Bay) to all trade, essentially cutting Storm's End off.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_fall_of_the_dragons:_robert.27s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

The siege continued for the better part of a year, with the garrison having to eat their [horses](wiki:Horse), [dogs](wiki:Dog) and [cats](wiki:Cat), and were nearly forced to eat their own dead.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) [Donal Noye](wiki:Donal_Noye) lost an arm during the siege(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Ragot19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) after an axe wound festered. Meanwhile, Mace and his bannermen, such as Paxter and Lord [Mathis Rowan](wiki:Mathis_Rowan),(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racok33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) feasted within sight of the walls.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) Some of Mace's strength joined the army of Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen).(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-SSM_7-0)

There was at least one breach in discipline within Storm's End, when the castle's master-at-arms, Ser [Gawen Wylde](wiki:Gawen_Wylde), and three knights were caught trying to sneak out a postern gate to surrender. [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) prepared to have them hurled at the [Tyrell](wiki:House_Tyrell) host via catapult. Maester [Cressen](wiki:Cressen) convinced Stannis to imprison the men, however, since the garrison might need to eat the dead if their food ran out. Gawen eventually died in his cell during the siege.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racok33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1)

[Davos](wiki:Davos_Seaworth), a notorious smuggler, eventually slipped through the Redwyne fleet's lines one night and entered a tunnel beneath Storm's End(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racok42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) with a ship loaded with onions and salt fish for the starving garrison.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) This food allowed the garrison to survive long enough for Robert's friend and ally, Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), to march south after the [Battle of the Trident](wiki:Battle_of_the_Trident), in which Rhaegar was killed by Stannis's elder brother [Robert](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), and the ensuing [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing).(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

With King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) dead and the surviving Targaryens fled to [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)), Mace dipped his banners as soon as Eddard and the rebels appeared on the field outside Storm's End.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Ragot39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

## Aftermath

According to a semi-canon source, the successful defense of [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) prevented [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) and [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) from falling into loyalist hands, and it helped the rebel [storm lords](wiki:Stormlands) remain loyal to their liege, [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon).(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-SSM_7-1)

After Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell) surrendered, Stannis was ordered by the new king, his brother Robert I, to build [a new fleet](wiki:Royal_fleet) and [assault Dragonstone](wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone).(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4) The majority of the Reach's lords and knights were later pardoned by Robert for their part in the war.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

Stannis granted [Davos](wiki:Davos_Seaworth) a [knighthood](wiki:Knight) for assisting the garrison, as well as a keep and lands on [Cape Wrath](wiki:Cape_Wrath), [a war galley](wiki:Black_Betha) to be commanded by himself, and later two more to be commanded by his two eldest sons.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5) Stannis personally removed the first joint of each finger on Davos Seaworth's left hand as punishment for his smuggling past, however.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6) Davos's younger son [Devan](wiki:Devan_Seaworth) would later be taken as a squire by Stannis.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Racok10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

Having lost an arm, the blacksmith [Donal Noye](wiki:Donal_Noye) left Storm's End to join the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch).(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Ragot19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) During a visit to [Greenstone](wiki:Greenstone), King Robert slept with a widowed [Estermont cousin](wiki:Estermont_(cousin_of_Robert)) whose husband and father died during the siege.(wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End.cite_ref-Raffc24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

## Aftermath

[Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy) surrendered, his two eldest sons [Rodrik](wiki:Rodrik_Greyjoy) and [Maron](wiki:Maron_Greyjoy) were killed, and his last surviving son, [Theon](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), was made a ward of [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark) as hostage. [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon)'s victory cemented his hold on the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), having overthrown the [Targaryens](wiki:House_Targaryen) six years before. He threw a great [tourney at Lannisport](wiki:Tourney_at_Lannisport) to celebrate his victory.(wiki:Greyjoy's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-2)

The rebellion resulted in the [castle of Pyke](wiki:Pyke) being severely damaged and partially rebuilt. The [Faith of the Seven](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven) suffered abuse in the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) at the hands of fanatics who worship the [Drowned God](wiki:Drowned_God). [Baelor Blacktyde](wiki:Baelor_Blacktyde), [Lord of Blacktyde](wiki:Lord_of_Blacktyde), was a child when [his father](wiki:Lord_Blacktyde) died in the war. As a result he was taken away to [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown) as a hostage following the end of the rebellion. He returned eight years later as a follower of the Seven.(wiki:Greyjoy's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)

## Origins

In [42 AC](wiki:42_AC), Jaehaerys's father, King [Aenys I Targaryen](wiki:Aenys_I_Targaryen), died on [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone). Dowager Queen [Visenya](wiki:Visenya_Targaryen) brought her son and Aenys's half-brother, Prince [Maegor](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen), back from his exile in [Essos](wiki:Essos), and he declared himself king, supplanting Aenys's eldest son and heir, Prince [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I)). In [43 AC](wiki:43_AC), Aegon attempted [a rebellion](wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion) against his usurping uncle, but he and his [dragon](wiki:Dragon) [Quicksilver](wiki:Quicksilver) were slain by Maegor and his dragon [Balerion](wiki:Balerion) in a [battle](wiki:Battle_Beneath_the_Gods_Eye) just south of the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye).(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

By [44 AC](wiki:44_AC), Prince Jaehaerys, his sister [Alysanne](wiki:Alysanne_Targaryen), and their mother, Dowager Queen [Alyssa Velaryon](wiki:Alyssa_Velaryon), resided on Dragonstone as the [hostages](wiki:Hostage) of Dowager Queen Visenya. In the chaos following Visenya's death of natural causes that year, Jaehaerys and his mother and sister fled the island, along with Jaehaerys's dragon [Vermithor](wiki:Vermithor) and Alysanne's dragon [Silverwing](wiki:Silverwing), and taking Visenya's ancestral [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) sword [Dark Sister](wiki:Dark_Sister). In retaliation, King Maegor had Jaehaerys's older brother [Viserys](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I)), a hostage in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), [tortured](wiki:Torture) to death. As a result, Prince Jaehaerys became the eldest living son of the late King Aenys, though he remained in hiding.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

King Maegor had great difficulty getting a heir of his own body. Two of his wives, Queen [Ceryse Hightower](wiki:Ceryse_Hightower) and Queen [Tyanna](wiki:Tyanna), appeared to be barren, and in [44 AC](wiki:44_AC) he had Queen [Alys Harroway](wiki:Alys_Harroway) tortured to death after she had given birth to a stillborn monstrosity. In [47 AC](wiki:47_AC), Maegor forced three widows of proven fertility to marry him in one ceremony. One of these [Black Brides](wiki:Black_Brides) was Jaehaerys's eldest sister and Aegon's widow, [Rhaena Targaryen](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)). Still without a child of his own, Maegor officially disinherited Jaehaerys, and proclaimed Rhaena and Aegon's daughter [Aerea](wiki:Aerea_Targaryen) as his heir.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

In the meanwhile, the [Faith of the Seven](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven) had been conflicting with [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) over their practices of [incest](wiki:Incest) and [polygamy](wiki:Polygamy). The [Faith Militant uprising](wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising) had begun during King Aenys's reign,(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) but as the Faith continued to resist Maegor's rule, he raised [a set of laws](wiki:King_Maegor's_laws) that forbade holy men from carrying arms,(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Raffc43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_jaehaerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) and he punished [lords](wiki:Lord) who spoke out against the suppression of the Faith.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) King Maegor took brutal steps to put down the rebelling [Faith Militant](wiki:Faith_Militant) orders and the houses supporting them, including offering bounties for their heads.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Raffc33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Not even the death of the [High Septon](wiki:High_Septon_(Maegor_I)) and his replacement by [a more passive one](wiki:High_Lickspittle) stopped the determination of the Faith Militant. Thousands died during Maegor's campaigns against the Faith, which was also ruinously expensive. The [master of coin](wiki:Master_of_coin) continuously raised taxes and imposed new ones, and when these measures failed, Maegor demanded a tribute of [gold](wiki:Gold) from the high lords. Day by day, the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms) turned against its king, as both [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) and lords began to despise him for his tyranny and his many cruelties.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

## Aftermath

Jaehaerys arrived in King's Landing upon his [dragon](wiki:Dragon) [Vermithor](wiki:Vermithor), ahead of Lord Rogar Baratheon's army. Most of Maegor's supporters had fled, and Jaehaerys imprisoned the few who still remained. After he was joined by his mother and Hand, King Jaehaerys decided to pardon the lords and knights who had served Maegor, restoring their lands and titles to them, although he required a son or daughter as a [hostage](wiki:Hostage) from each, or the payment of a sum of [gold](wiki:Gold) to the crown. As the realm learned about Jaehaerys's willingness for granting pardons, the remainder of Maegor's adherents dismissed their armies and journeyed to King's Landing to swear fealty to the new king. An exception was made for some: the gaolers and [confessors](wiki:Confessor) who had assisted in the [torture](wiki:Torture) and death of Prince [Viserys](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I)) were all executed.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabprince_into_king_-_the_ascension_of_jaehaerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)

Of Maegor's Kingsguard, Ser [Maladon Moore](wiki:Maladon_Moore) was executed for his alleged involvement in the murder of Maegor's queen [Ceryse Hightower](wiki:Ceryse_Hightower), and his definite particpation in the murder of Queen [Tyanna](wiki:Tyanna_of_the_Tower). The remaining five Kingsguard were given a choice between execution or [taking the black](wiki:Night's_Watch), including Ser [Olyver Bracken](wiki:Olyver_Bracken) and Ser [Raymund Mallery](wiki:Raymund_Mallery), who had abandoned Maegor for Jaehaerys, but whose service was refused by the young king since he did not want oathbreakers serving him. All chose to take the black, except for Ser [Harrold Langward](wiki:Harrold_Langward), who demanded a [trial by combat](wiki:Trial_by_combat) and was slain by the Crown's champion, Ser [Gyles Morrigen](wiki:Gyles_Morrigen). Afterwards, Ser Gyles was made [Lord Commander](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Kingsguard) of Jaehaerys's Kingsguard.(wiki:Jaehaerys_Targaryen's_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabprince_into_king_-_the_ascension_of_jaehaerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

## Origins

Following the [fall of Astapor](wiki:Fall_of_Astapor), [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) marches north along the coast of [Slaver's Bay](wiki:Slaver's_Bay), towards the coastal city of [Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai), the second great [slaver](wiki:Slave) city.

The Yunkish host holds the middle with a 4,000 strong infantry slave army, supported by two mounted [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) companies in the flanks: the [Stormcrows](wiki:Stormcrows) on the left and the [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons) on the right. Both companies have about 500 men each.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

Daenerys's host numbers 10,000 [Unsullied](wiki:Unsullied) and several thousand ill-trained armed [freedmen](wiki:Freedmen). Her forces are stronger both numerically and qualitatively and could easily defeat the Yunkai army. Nevertheless Daenerys, worrying about the cost, extends an invitation to the slavers and the leaders of the sellsword companies to meet with her separately, trying to turn the sellswords over to her side and lure the [Wise Masters](wiki:Wise_Masters) of Yunkai to release the city's slaves without a fight.

Speaking for the Stormcrows' captains, [Prendahl na Ghezn](wiki:Prendahl_na_Ghezn) angrily rejects Daenerys's offer to change allegiance. [Mero](wiki:Mero) of the Second Sons is willing to take the offer back to his own captains, so Daenerys gifts him a [wagon](wiki:Wagon) full of [wine](wiki:Wine). [Grazdan mo Eraz](wiki:Grazdan_mo_Eraz) of Yunkai offers a bribe to Daenerys to leave but is refused.

The battle plan is outlined by Daenerys to her advisors: a surprise attack on both enemy flanks by the Unsullied, led by their captain, [Grey Worm](wiki:Grey_Worm), and Ser [Jorah Mormont](wiki:Jorah_Mormont), the commander of her army. Her *[kos](wiki:Ko)*—[Aggo](wiki:Aggo), [Jhogo](wiki:Jhogo), and [Rakharo](wiki:Rakharo)—are to lead her mounted warriors of about 30 men in wedge formation against the enemy center. [Arstan Whitebeard](wiki:Arstan_Whitebeard) and [Strong Belwas](wiki:Belwas) are held back to guard her pavilion during the battle.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

Near midnight the Unsullied catch [Daario Naharis](wiki:Daario_Naharis) of the Stormcrows approaching the camp. He reveals he has killed the other captains, Prendahl and [Sallor the Bald](wiki:Sallor), and he surrenders the Stormcrows over to her. He pledges his sword and Daenerys accepts, aware that she risks being betrayed. Before he returns to his men, he is told to attack the Yunkish in the rear when the attack begins.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

## Narrative Arc

The attack is launched a few hours after midnight, and the [Stormcrows](wiki:Stormcrows) attack the enemy in the rear, as promised. The [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons) are too drunk from wine to fight and are unable to support the slave army. Surrounded from all directions, the [Yunkish](wiki:Yunkai) troops break and throw down their weapons, while the sellswords yield.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Almost 200 Yunkish infantry are killed and the rest are taken captive. The Second Sons suffer light casualties, but their captain [Mero](wiki:Mero) flees when he realizes that the Stormcrows have betrayed them. [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen)'s losses are minimal, with only a dozen men.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

## Aftermath

[Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai) surrenders and gives in to [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen)'s demands, freeing all the slaves and allowing each of them whatever wealth they can carry from the [Wise Masters](wiki:Wise_Masters). In return they are allowed to remained in control of the city, which also remains unsacked. The freed slaves swell the ranks of Daenerys's freedmen and cement her reputation as the "[Mother](wiki:Mhysa)" among her followers. She proceeds to [Meereen](wiki:Meereen), the last great slaver city.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5) Now under the command of [Brown Ben Plumm](wiki:Ben_Plumm), the [Second Sons](wiki:Second_Sons) join the [Stormcrows](wiki:Stormcrows) as sellswords in the [Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) host.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Rasos57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

As their wealth remains mostly untouched, the Wise Masters of Yunkai are able to hire other sellsword companies and send envoys to [Qarth](wiki:Qarth), [Mantarys](wiki:Mantarys), [Elyria](wiki:Elyria), [Tolos](wiki:Tolos), [New Ghis](wiki:New_Ghis) and [Volantis](wiki:Volantis), forming an alliance to cast Daenerys down and restore the slave trade that her campaign has affected.(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Radwd16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Battle_near_Yunkai.cite_ref-Radwd63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

## Origins

Ser [Criston Cole](wiki:Criston_Cole), the [Lord Commander of the Kingsguard](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Kingsguard) and the new [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) to [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen), devised a more aggressive strategy for the [greens](wiki:Greens) than his predecessor as Hand, Ser [Otto Hightower](wiki:Otto_Hightower). Criston planned a trap for the [blacks](wiki:Blacks) at [Rook's Rest](wiki:Rook's_Rest), while also forcing the submission of the nearby [crownlands](wiki:Crownlands) houses that were allied with Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen).(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

Criston Cole marched out of [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) with 100 [knights](wiki:Knight), 500 [men-at-arms](wiki:Men-at-arms), and three times as many [sellswords](wiki:Sellsword): thus about 2,400 in total. [Lord Rosby](wiki:Lord_Rosby) and [Lord Stokeworth](wiki:Lord_Stokeworth) had recently submitted to Aegon to avoid execution in King's Landing, and Criston forced them to prove their loyalty by adding the strength of [Rosby](wiki:Rosby) and [Stokeworth](wiki:Stokeworth) to the green host. Thus augmented, the army advanced upon [Duskendale](wiki:Duskendale). The walled harbor town was caught completely by surprise and unprepared, and quickly fell to Aegon II's forces and [was sacked](wiki:Sack_of_Duskendale). Lord [Gunthor Darklyn](wiki:Gunthor_Darklyn) was beheaded for [treason](wiki:Treason), and most of his [household knights](wiki:Household_knight) swore their swords to Aegon, although a few chose death.(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

[Lord Staunton](wiki:Lord_Staunton) had been forewarned of the approach of Ser Criston's army. He closed the gates of Rook's Rest, but was unable to prevent Criston from burning his fields or killing his [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) and livestock. When supplies in Rook's Rest began to run low, Lord Staunton asked for assistance from Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), sending a [raven](wiki:Raven) to [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone).(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

## Narrative Arc

Queen Rhaenyra's sons, [Jacaerys](wiki:Jacaerys_Velaryon) and [Joffrey Velaryon](wiki:Joffrey_Velaryon), were eager to join the fight atop their [dragons](wiki:Dragon), [Vermax](wiki:Vermax) and [Tyraxes](wiki:Tyraxes), but she forbade them to go. Instead, nine days after Staunton's message, [Rhaenys Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon)), the Queen Who Never Was, and her [dragon](wiki:Dragon), [Meleys](wiki:Meleys), the Red Queen, arrived alone above Rook's Rest to aid Lord Staunton. However, Criston was prepared, and had his archers and scorpions fire at Meleys. The dragon was largely unharmed by these attacks, and she responded by burning Criston's soldiers with [dragonfire](wiki:Dragonfire).(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Criston Cole then sprung his trap. King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) and his brother, Prince [Aemond](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen), appeared in the sky atop their own dragons, [Sunfyre](wiki:Sunfyre) and [Vhagar](wiki:Vhagar), and attacked Rhaenys and Meleys. The three dragons fought a thousand feet above the field. When Meleys closed her jaws around Sunfyre's neck, Vhagar fell upon them from above, causing the three dragons to crash into to the ground. Although Aemond and Vhagar rose unscathed, Meleys was broken and ripped to pieces from the fall, and Sunfyre had one wing half torn from his body. Aegon suffered severe burns and broken bones. The blackened body of Rhaenys was found beside the carcass of her dragon.(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

Aemond, Criston, and the surviving greens took Rook's Rest, and killed Lord Staunton and his garrison of one hundred soldiers.(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

## Aftermath

The heads of [Lord Staunton](wiki:Lord_Staunton) and Meleys were taken to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). The [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) of the city were greatly awed by the sight of the dragon's head, drawn through the city on a cart. Thousands of people left the city, until Dowager Queen [Alicent Hightower](wiki:Alicent_Hightower) ordered the gates closed and barred.

Ser [Criston Cole](wiki:Criston_Cole) lost eight hundred soldiers to Meleys's dragonfire. King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) was incapacitated by his burns for a year; Prince [Aemond Targaryen](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen) became [regent](wiki:Regent) while his brother recovered. Aegon's injured dragon, [Sunfyre](wiki:Sunfyre), was flightless and too heavy to be moved, so was left behind in the fields of Rook's Rest to eat carcasses and livestock.

The loss of [Rhaenys Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aemon)) and her dragon, [Meleys](wiki:Meleys), was a severe blow to the [blacks](wiki:Blacks). Rhaenys's husband, Lord [Corlys Velaryon](wiki:Corlys_Velaryon), was enraged, and blamed her death on Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), stating that if the queen or her sons had gone with their dragons, his wife might be alive.

Lord [Walys Mooton](wiki:Walys_Mooton) later led the [retaking of Rook's Rest](wiki:Retaking_of_Rook's_Rest) for the blacks, but was slain in a failed attempt to kill Sunfyre.(wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

## Origins

With the majority of the northern lords and their hosts fighting in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) and the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) of southern [Westeros](wiki:Westeros), the [north](wiki:North) is left relatively undefended. Lord [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy) declares himself [King of the Isles and the North](wiki:King_of_the_Isles_and_the_North) and orders his longships to take the north by conquest. His son, [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), is sent to [harry the Stony Shore](wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore) with [Dagmer Cleftjaw](wiki:Dagmer). After completing this task, the two plot the capture of [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell), the ancestral seat of [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark). Dagmer besieges [Torrhen's Square](wiki:Torrhen's_Square), the seat of the [Tallharts](wiki:House_Tallhart), in order to draw out the garrison of Winterfell, leaving the castle lightly defended.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Theon [captures Winterfell](wiki:Capture_of_Winterfell) in one night.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) After some of his prisoners, including [Bran](wiki:Bran_Stark) and [Rickon Stark](wiki:Rickon_Stark), disappear, however, Theon presents the burned bodies of two sons of a miller as those of the missing Stark princes.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok50.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

Ser [Rodrik Cassel](wiki:Rodrik_Cassel), the castellan of Winterfell, breaks Dagmer's force in the [fight at Torrhen's Square](wiki:Fight_at_Torrhen's_Square). Strengthened by Tallhart men, he marches on Winterfell from [Castle Cerwyn](wiki:Castle_Cerwyn).(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) Many of the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) with Theon abandon him, including [Urzen](wiki:Urzen), [Stygg](wiki:Stygg), and ten of [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy)'s men, leaving Theon with only seventeen remaining in his command.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9)(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Theon_8-0) However, Theon has allowed a [House Bolton](wiki:House_Bolton) serving man, "[Reek](wiki:Reek)", to leave Winterfell in search of aid by promising to find a few hundred reinforcements for Theon.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)

The Stark army assembled by Rodrik totals less than two thousand men.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-11) It includes six hundred from Winterfell and its vicinity under the command of Rodrik Cassel, three hundred [Cerwyns](wiki:House_Cerwyn) under the command of young Lord [Cley Cerwyn](wiki:Cley_Cerwyn), and a force of Tallharts under the command of [Leobald Tallhart](wiki:Leobald_Tallhart). Banners from Houses [Flint](wiki:House_Flint), [Hornwood](wiki:House_Hornwood), [Karstark](wiki:House_Karstark), and [Manderly](wiki:House_Manderly) are also seen.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-12) Lord [Wyman Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly) sent a dozen barges packed with [knights](wiki:Knight), warhorses, and siege engines up the [White Knife](wiki:White_Knife).(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3) At Winterfell Theon sees northern catapults and scorpions, but no siege towers, although there is plenty of timber in the [wolfswood](wiki:Wolfswood) if need be.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-13)

## Aftermath

Although the battle at Winterfell is not especially bloody,(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Rssm1284.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) the hold of [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark) on the north is shattered. Most of Ser [Rodrik Cassel](wiki:Rodrik_Cassel)'s leaders are killed, but a good many of his common soldiers escape and return to their villages and holdfasts. However, their accounts of what happened vary.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Rssm1116.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

According to [Ramsay Snow](wiki:Ramsay_Snow), [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) cut down Rodrik during a parley.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Radwd29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) At the [Twins](wiki:Twins) prior to the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding), Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton) tells [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), the [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North), that his son Ramsay successfully rallied the northmen after Theon's ironmen cut down Rodrik and [Leobald Tallhart](wiki:Leobald_Tallhart).(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Rasos49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) [Wex Pyke](wiki:Wex_Pyke) indicates to Lord [Wyman Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly) and [Robett Glover](wiki:Robett_Glover) that Ramsay's claims are false.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Radwd29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1)

After the [fight by Deepwood Motte](wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte), [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon)'s army is joined by [Cerwyns](wiki:House_Cerwyn), [Hornwoods](wiki:House_Hornwood), and [Tallharts](wiki:House_Tallhart) who survived the battle outside the gates of Winterfell.(wiki:Battle_outside_the_gates_of_Winterfell.cite_ref-Radwd35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

## Origins

### Baelor I and Viserys II

After King [Baelor I Targaryen](wiki:Baelor_I_Targaryen) made peace with [Dorne](wiki:Dorne), he returned to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) and annulled his marriage to his eldest sister, [Daena](wiki:Daena_Targaryen). He then locked Daena with his other sisters, [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_III)) and [Elaena](wiki:Elaena_Targaryen), in the [Maidenvault](wiki:Maidenvault), so they could not tempt the lusts of men. However, Daena contrived to escape her imprisonment on several occasions, and towards the end of her brother's reign became pregnant by her cousin, Prince [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) In late [170 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Daena gave birth to a [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son whom she named [Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Daena refused to reveal the identity of the father of her child, though Aegon was already suspected to have been involved.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) King Baelor, in response to Daemon's birth, began a forty-day fast, which eventually caused his death in [171 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_baelor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2) After the claims of Daena and her sisters were dismissed, Daena's uncle was crowned as King [Viserys II Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_II_Targaryen). Viserys ruled only for a year, until his own death, after which his eldest son, King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen), ascended the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) in [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_viserys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

### Aegon IV

In addition to his unacknowledged bastard son by [Daena](wiki:Daena_Targaryen), King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen) had by his marriage to his sister, [Naerys](wiki:Naerys_Targaryen), a trueborn son named [Daeron](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), born in [153 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), and a daughter, [Daenerys](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV)), born in [172 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest). Aegon often clashed with Prince Daeron during his reign, with Daeron opposing the ideas and the corrupt style of rule of his father. Aegon, on the other hand, despised his sister-wife, Naerys, and his younger brother, Prince [Aemon the Dragonknight](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Viserys_II)), to both of whom Prince Daeron was close. When Aegon, in [174 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), planned to launch an unprovoked attack against [Dorne](wiki:Dorne), the homeland of his daughter-in-law [Myriah Martell](wiki:Myriah_Martell), Daeron opposed his ideas. Around the same time, rumors began to circulate, instigated by King Aegon himself, that Daeron was a bastard born of adultery between his siblings, Naerys and Aemon. For the first time, though not the last, Aegon threatened to name one of his bastard sons ([Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) and [Balerion Otherys](wiki:Balerion_Otherys) being the only sons Aegon had, at this point, acknowledged) as his heir over Daeron.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2) The rumors about Daeron's parentage forever remained, but despite Aegon's threats and japes, Daeron was never formally disowned. Meanwhile, two factions began to form at court, one supporting the corrupt reign of Aegon IV, the other supporting Daeron.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)

In [182 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), Aegon knighted his bastard son, [Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), after the boy won a squire's tourney at the age of twelve. Aegon publicly bestowed [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), one of the [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) swords of [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen), on Daemon as well,(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-4) finally acknowledging him.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-greatbastards_13-0) There were some who felt that the sword symbolized the monarchy(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-14) (not unlike rumors that were spread when King [Aenys I Targaryen](wiki:Aenys_I_Targaryen) bestowed the sword on his half-brother, Prince [Maegor](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen)),(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-15) as it had been the sword of King [Aegon I Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_I_Targaryen), the Conqueror, and had been passed on from king to king ever since. Aegon's public gift of the sword eventually started talk that Daemon should be king.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-targaryenhistory_16-0) Following the acquisition of the sword, Daemon took the name "[Blackfyre](wiki:House_Blackfyre)" for himself.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iv.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-5)

### Daeron II

In early [184 AC](wiki:184_AC), King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen) legitimized all of his bastards on his deathbed. Prince Daeron ascended the throne as King [Daeron II Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), and did all he could to keep the nobly-born [Great Bastards](wiki:Great_Bastards) close. He paid the [dowry](wiki:Dowry) his late father had promised to the [Archon of Tyrosh](wiki:Archon_of_Tyrosh), seeing his half-brother [Daemon I Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre) wed to [Rohanne of Tyrosh](wiki:Rohanne_of_Tyrosh), as per the late Aegon's desire.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Daeron was a cultured and scholarly man who swept the court clear of Aegon the Unworthy's corruption, depriving several people of their advantageous positions. Daeron placed men of his own in the positions of power, and he began negotiations with his brother-in-law, [Maron Martell](wiki:Maron_Martell), the [Prince of Dorne](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne). This resulted in the [wedding](wiki:Wedding_of_Maron_Martell_and_Daenerys_Targaryen) of Maron to Daeron's younger sister, Princess [Daenerys](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon_IV)), in [187 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), and the joining of [Dorne](wiki:Dorne) into the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms).(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

Dissatisfaction grew from the fact that Dorne had won several concessions during Maron's negotiations that the other regions did not have (e.g. the right to keep their [royal title](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne), autonomy to maintain their own laws, the right to assess and gather the taxes due to the Iron Throne with only irregular oversight from the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep)). Additionally, it was the belief that the Dornish held too much power at court, as many Dornishmen were brought to Daeron's court, and several filled important offices.(wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

## Origins

After the [fall of Astapor](wiki:Fall_of_Astapor), [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) free the city's [slaves](wiki:Slaves) and names a council to rule the city. [Cleon](wiki:Cleon) slays the council after Daenerys departs, however, and proclaims himself king.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Rasos71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

[Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai), one of the remaining [Slaver Cities](wiki:Slaver_Cities), declares war on Astapor, and the Yunkai'i hire several [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) companies, including the [Long Lances](wiki:Long_Lances), the [Company of the Cat](wiki:Company_of_the_Cat), and the [Windblown](wiki:Windblown).(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) The Yunkish are also joined by two [iron legions](wiki:Iron_legions) from [New Ghis](wiki:New_Ghis), and Cleon is defeated in a [battle at the Horns of Hazzat](wiki:Battle_at_the_Horns_of_Hazzat).(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

The armies of the Yunkai'i descend on Astapor.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) When Cleon commands the Astapori to march out against the Yunkai'i, the Butcher King is slain by his own soldiers. Another man declares himself [Cleon the Second](wiki:Cleon_the_Second), but his throat is cut eight days later. That man's killer claims the crown, as does the first Cleon's concubine. The supporters of the rival monarchs fight within Astapor.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

Meanwhile, the forces of Yunkai and its allies besiege Astapor, devour the Astapori crops, and slaughter their herds. Inside, the Astapori starve; [King Cutthroat](wiki:King_Cutthroat) and [Queen Whore](wiki:Queen_Whore) accuse each other of feasting on the flesh of the slain. Men and women gather in secret to draw lots and gorge upon the flesh of those who draw a black stone. The [pyramid of Nakloz](wiki:Pyramid_of_Nakloz) is despoiled and set aflame by those who claim that [Kraznys mo Nakloz](wiki:Kraznys_mo_Nakloz) is to blame for all their woes. Others blame Daenerys but many still love her. There are rumors that King Cleon sent for Daenerys and she is coming at the head of a great host, with food for all. There are other rumors that she has been seen mounted on a [dragon](wiki:Dragon) flying high above the camps of the Yunkai'i.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

Inside the desperate city the [Green Grace](wiki:Green_Grace_(Astapor)) has a vision that dead King Cleon will deliver Astapor from the Yunkai. The despairing Astapori heed this vision and attempt to break the siege, making the decision to have Cleon's corpse disinterred and strapped onto the back of a starving horse to lead Astapor's new and inferior [Unsullied](wiki:Unsullied) on a sortie outside the city's gates.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1)

## Narrative Arc

The fight beneath the walls of [Astapor](wiki:Astapor) begins at dawn when the new [Unsullied](wiki:Unsullied) march out of the gates in a sortie against the forces of [Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai) and its allies and [sellswords](wiki:Sellsword). Some of the [Windblown](wiki:Windblown) are asleep when the battle commences so they must quickly don their armor. Three hundred yards away Astapor's Unsullied pour through the gates and form up in ranks beneath Astapor's crumbling red brick walls. Having joined the mercenaries in disguise, Prince [Quentyn Martell](wiki:Quentyn_Martell), Ser [Archibald Yronwood](wiki:Archibald_Yronwood), and Ser [Gerris Drinkwater](wiki:Gerris_Drinkwater) join the Windblown horse line as drums beat in the distance. Gerris points out to Quentyn the Butcher King, sitting tall upon an armored horse in a suit of [copper](wiki:Copper) scale that flashes brilliantly in the [sun](wiki:Sun).(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

As the Yunkishmen run about in *[tokars](wiki:Tokar)* trying to arrange their half-trained slave soldiers, Unsullied spears crash through the siege lines. The Windblown and the [Company of the Cat](wiki:Company_of_the_Cat) are ahorse in minutes, however, and they thunder down on the Astapori flanks. At the same time, an [iron legion](wiki:Iron_legion) from [New Ghis](wiki:New_Ghis) pushes through the Yunkish camp from the other side and meets the Unsullied spear to spear and shield to shield. The rest of the battle is butchery.

[Caggo](wiki:Caggo) cuts the Butcher King down, fighting through the king's protectors on his monstrous warhorse and opening Cleon the Great from shoulder to hip with one blow of his curved [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) *[arakh](wiki:Arakh)*. Cleon's fall breaks the Astapori Unsullied; they throw down their spears and shields and run, only to find the gates of Astapor shut behind them. The Windblown ride down and slaughter the frightened eunuchs, slashing right and left through the Unsullied.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

After the defeat of their Unsullied outside the walls, the Astapori impale the Green Grace upon a stake in the [Plaza of Punishment](wiki:Plaza_of_Punishment) and leave her until she dies. In the [pyramid of Ullhor](wiki:Pyramid_of_Ullhor), survivors hold a great feast with [poisoned](wiki:Poison) wine in the night so they will die by the morning. The [bloody flux](wiki:Bloody_flux) then kills three men in every four.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)

A mob of Astapori eventually slay the guards and open the main gate, allowing the legions to come pouring into Astapor, followed by the [Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai)'i and the [sellswords](wiki:Sellsword) on their horses. [Queen Whore](wiki:Queen_Whore) is killed in the fighting, and [King Cutthroat](wiki:King_Cutthroat) is torn apart by dogs in a [fighting pit](wiki:Fighting_pit).(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3) The legions seal the doors of the [Temple of the Graces](wiki:Temple_of_the_Graces_(Astapor)), which is full of sick Astapori, and set the building afire. The blaze spreads throughout Astapor, however.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4) There is no way out, for the Yunkai'i have sealed the broken gates to keep the dead and dying inside the city.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)

## Aftermath

The [Tattered Prince](wiki:Tattered_Prince) takes the lion's share of the [Windblown](wiki:Windblown)'s Astapori plunder.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4) [Caggo](wiki:Caggo) becomes known as *Corpsekiller* for having cut down the already-dead [Cleon](wiki:Cleon) in battle, although he does not care for the name. The allied hosts march north to [Yunkai](wiki:Yunkai) to take provisions before continuing on to [Meereen](wiki:Meereen).(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5)

Hundreds or thousands of Astapori survivors eventually pour into the countryside, but many have the bloody flux. The Yunkai'i command their sellswords to hunt the survivors and drive them back to Astapor or north to Meereen to prevent the Astapori from going near the Yellow City of Yunkai.(wiki:Siege_of_Astapor.cite_ref-Radwd30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5)

## Origins

King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) impregnated Queen [Rhaella Targaryen](wiki:Rhaella_Targaryen) during [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion), his ardor raised after burning his [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), Lord [Qarlton Chelsted](wiki:Qarlton_Chelsted), for opposing the [wildfire plot](wiki:Wildfire_plot).(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) After the Mad King learned that Crown Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) had been killed by Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) in the [Battle of the Trident](wiki:Battle_of_the_Trident), he sent Rhaella with Prince [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen) to [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)), the ancient island stronghold of [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen).(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Rasos37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Years later, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) remembers Rhaella traveling from [Aegon's High Hill](wiki:Aegon's_High_Hill) to a ship in the morning,(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) while Viserys tells his sister [Daenerys](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) tales of a midnight flight to Dragonstone.(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

The royal family who remained in King's Landing, including Aerys and Rhaegar's children, were slain in the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing) in [283 AC](wiki:283_AC). With Rhaella and Viserys at Dragonstone, Robert Baratheon succeeded Aerys on the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) as [Lord of the Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Lord_of_the_Seven_Kingdoms). King Robert tasked his oldest brother, [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), who had successfully withstood the [siege of Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End) during the rebellion, with building a new fleet for [House Baratheon of King's Landing](wiki:House_Baratheon_of_King's_Landing).(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

Aerys's widow, Rhaella, died giving birth to Daenerys nine months later at the castle of [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone), the last stronghold held by the Targaryens. A ferocious storm raged that night and destroyed the Targaryen fleet while it lay at anchor.(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Narrative Arc

Commanding from the decks of the war galley *[Fury](wiki:Fury)*,(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racok58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) Stannis set sail with the new [royal fleet](wiki:Royal_fleet) to capture Dragonstone. The garrison of its castle was ready to sell Viserys and the infant Daenerys to the Baratheons. However, just before Stannis sailed, Ser [Willem Darry](wiki:Willem_Darry), the former [master-at-arms](wiki:Master-at-arms) of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep), led four other loyalists in smuggling Viserys, Daenerys, and a [wet nurse](wiki:Wet_nurse) from Dragonstone under cover of night. They sailed for the [Braavosian Coastland](wiki:Braavosian_Coastland) across the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea).(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)

Stannis's assault on Dragonstone was successful,(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racok58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) and he conquered the island and its castle in the name of King Robert I Baratheon.(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

## Aftermath

With the conquest of [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone), the [Targaryens](wiki:House_Targaryen) lost their last holding in the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms).(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3) [Viserys](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen) and [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) lived in exile with Ser [Willem Darry](wiki:Willem_Darry) at the [house with the red door](wiki:House_with_the_red_door) in [Braavos](wiki:Braavos),(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) until Darry's death.(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-4)

King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) granted Dragonstone to [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), making him Lord of Dragonstone. The island castle was traditionally the seat of the crown prince, the [Prince of Dragonstone](wiki:Prince_of_Dragonstone), so Robert honored Stannis by granting him the seat of the heir to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1)(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-8) However, Robert was also furious that the Targaryen children had escaped before Stannis arrived, and he granted the ancestral [Baratheon](wiki:House_Baratheon) seat of [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) to the youngest brother, [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon), which Stannis resented as an intentional slight.(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2)(wiki:Assault_on_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Racok25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

## Origins

### Background

During the [First Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion), the loyalist forces of King [Daeron II Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen) defeated the rebel forces of his half-brother, [Daemon I Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), one of the legitimized [Great Bastards](wiki:Great_Bastards) of King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen). Daemon was killed in the [Battle of the Redgrass Field](wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field), but Ser [Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers), another Great Bastard and one of Daemon's strongest supporters, was able to escape to the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) with Daemon's surviving family and [Blackfyre](wiki:Blackfyre), the famed [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) sword.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

Many of those who had fought for the Black Dragon in the rebellion had their children taken to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) as hostages. Most of these hostages died during the [Great Spring Sickness](wiki:Great_Spring_Sickness), as did King Daeron, allowing the rebellious families to plot once more.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

### Wedding tourney

The marriage of Lord [Ambrose Butterwell](wiki:Ambrose_Butterwell) to [a daughter](wiki:Lady_Butterwell) of [Lord Frey](wiki:Lord_Frey) was used by some Blackfyre supporters to join their forces with those who resented the rule of Ser [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers), another Great Bastard and the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) to Daeron's son, King [Aerys I Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen). [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_II_Blackfyre), son of the late Daemon I, [dreamed](wiki:Dream) that a [dragon egg](wiki:Dragon_egg) would hatch, and Lord [Gormon Peake](wiki:Gormon_Peake) believed that a living [dragon](wiki:Dragon) would gain the rebels the support of the [Butterwells](wiki:House_Butterwell) of [Whitewalls](wiki:Whitewalls), the [Freys](wiki:House_Frey) of the [Twins](wiki:Twins), the [Lothstons](wiki:House_Lothston) of [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal), and the [Brackens](wiki:House_Bracken) of [Stone Hedge](wiki:Stone_Hedge). Ser [Tommard Heddle](wiki:Tommard_Heddle) was skeptical of Daemon's dreams, however, and he wished that they had the support of Aegor Rivers, as Bittersteel remained in [Essos](wiki:Essos) with the sword Blackfyre.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

The organizers of the new rebellion convinced Ambrose to name his prized dragon egg as the prize for the winner of his [wedding tourney at Whitewalls](wiki:Wedding_tourney_at_Whitewalls). Daemon entered the tourney disguised as a [hedge knight](wiki:Hedge_knight), Ser John the Fiddler, and the plotters attempted to rig the competition so that Daemon would win the egg. A drunken Daemon attempted to gain the support of Ser [Duncan the Tall](wiki:Duncan_the_Tall) during the festivities, with "John" telling Dunk he had dreamed of him in the white armor of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard).

Because of his prior involvement in the [tourney at Ashford Meadow](wiki:Tourney_at_Ashford_Meadow), Duncan decided to enter the tourney as a [mystery knight](wiki:Mystery_knight) called the Gallows Knight. He was defeated in his joust by Ser [Uthor Underleaf](wiki:Uthor_Underleaf), however. Uthor later pointed out to Duncan that one of the gold coins he had won had been coined during the Blackfyre Rebellion, and that an unknown party wanted him to kill Duncan during the competition.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)

### Rebellion

When the dragon egg disappeared from Lord Butterwell's bedchamber, Ser [Glendon Flowers](wiki:Glendon_Flowers) was framed by Lord Peake for the theft because of his refusal to lose to Daemon in the lists. Glendon was thrown in a cell when the missing egg was found in his possessions.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-4)

When Lord [Alyn Cockshaw](wiki:Alyn_Cockshaw), Daemon's friend, attempted to kill Duncan out of jealousy, Duncan kicked Alyn into a well to drown, an event observed by Ser [Maynard Plumm](wiki:Maynard_Plumm). Meanwhile, Duncan's squire Egg, actually Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_V_Targaryen), revealed his identity to Maester [Lothar](wiki:Lothar), who then informed Lords Butterwell and Frey. The Frey retinue immediately departed Whitewalls to return to the Twins, and Ambrose admitted his doubts about the conspiracy to Duncan and insisted that Lord Peake had plotted everything. Duncan then slew Ser Tommard in single combat when he tried to take Prince Aegon into captivity.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-5)

Within the great hall of Whitewalls, Duncan publicly accused John the Fiddler of being Daemon and alleged that the egg that had been found in Glendon's saddlebags was actually a painted stone planted by order of Lord Peake; the real dragon egg was still missing. Daemon had been unaware of the plots, and was unsure who to believe. He ordered a trial by joust, declaring that he would personally face Glendon.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-6)

Many wedding guests disappeared before the joust the next morning, including Ser Maynard. Daemon washed the black dye from his hair to reveal silver and gold [Valyrian](wiki:Valyrians) hair, and his horse bore the sigil of [House Blackfyre](wiki:House_Blackfyre). Glendon, who had been tortured in the dungeon, unhorsed Daemon on the first pass.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-7)

As the joust was ending, Lord [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers), called Bloodraven, arrived outside of Whitewalls with three of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), his three hundred [Raven's Teeth](wiki:Raven's_Teeth), and five hundred knights and five thousand spears from the [crownlands](wiki:Crownlands) and the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). These were drawn from Houses [Darklyn](wiki:House_Darklyn), [Hayford](wiki:House_Hayford), [Massey](wiki:House_Massey), [Rosby](wiki:House_Rosby), [Stokeworth](wiki:House_Stokeworth), [Mooton](wiki:House_Mooton), [Lothston](wiki:House_Lothston), and finally [House Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood), the house of Brynden's mother, [Melissa](wiki:Melissa_Blackwood). Daemon called for those within the castle to fight to the death but was laughed off by the fighting men. Daemon then marched out and offered single combat, but was promptly taken prisoner by Lord Rivers, thus ending the rebellion before it could begin. The only man killed was a servant of Lord [Vyrwel](wiki:House_Vyrwel) who revealed that he had been spying for Bloodraven and was then slain by a [Costayne](wiki:House_Costayne) knight.(wiki:Second_Blackfyre_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-8)

### Aftermath

Ser [Roland Crakehall](wiki:Roland_Crakehall_(Kingsguard)) informed Ser [Duncan the Tall](wiki:Duncan_the_Tall) that the conspirators would be taken to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) for trial. Soldiers following their liege's commands would presumably be lightly punished, and lords who told the truth would be pardoned after giving up a hostage. ## Origins

To restore the [Old Way](wiki:Old_Way),(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy), [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands), declared himself [King of the Iron Islands](wiki:King_of_the_Iron_Islands).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rawoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) [Greyjoy's Rebellion](wiki:Greyjoy's_Rebellion) began with the surprise [burning of the Lannister fleet](wiki:Burning_of_the_Lannister_fleet) at [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) The [storming of Seagard](wiki:Storming_of_Seagard) by Balon's [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) was a failure, however, with Balon's heir, [Rodrik Greyjoy](wiki:Rodrik_Greyjoy), killed by Lord [Jason Mallister](wiki:Jason_Mallister).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

Lord [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon)'s victory over the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) in the [sea battle off Fair Isle](wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle) allowed royal forces to land in the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands). Stannis subdued [Great Wyk](wiki:Great_Wyk),(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Radwd26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) led the invasion of [Old Wyk](wiki:Old_Wyk),(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rasos67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) and forces of King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) also landed on [Harlaw](wiki:Harlaw) and [Orkmont](wiki:Orkmont).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rawoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1) Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), [Warden of the West](wiki:Warden_of_the_West), also supported the invasions.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rawoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

With Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), [Warden of the North](wiki:Warden_of_the_North) and his childhood friend, beside him, Robert landed on the [isle of Pyke](wiki:Pyke_(island)). Balon fell back to his own [castle of Pyke](wiki:Pyke).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rawoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3) The castle of [House Botley](wiki:House_Botley) was destroyed by Robert in a [battle at Lordsport](wiki:Battle_at_Lordsport) before the main attack on the castle of Pyke was launched.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

## Narrative Arc

Thousands of men were with Robert and Eddard on Pyke.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) They assaulted Pyke's walls with [catapults](wiki:Catapult),(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3) and [Maron Greyjoy](wiki:Maron_Greyjoy), the second of Balon's three sons, was killed on the walls(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Ragotappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) when the south tower collapsed.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4) Balon's youngest son, [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), watched the fighting from the safety of the [Sea Tower](wiki:Sea_Tower).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5)

[Thoros of Myr](wiki:Thoros_of_Myr), a [red priest](wiki:Red_priest) in Robert's host, was first through the breach, wielding a sword coated in [wildfire](wiki:Wildfire).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Ragot27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) Lord [Jorah Mormont](wiki:Jorah_Mormont) was not far behind.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) Wielding [his warhammer](wiki:Robert's_warhammer), Robert entered the breach with Ned Stark by his side.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-6) The grandson of [Old Nan](wiki:Old_Nan) was killed on the walls.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Ragot24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

Ironborn resistance collapsed once the wall fell.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rawoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-4) Robert and Ned stood side by side for Balon's surrender in his fallen stronghold.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Balon reminded the [Lord of the Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Lord_of_the_Seven_Kingdoms) that he had never sworn him an oath after [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion). In return for his allegiance now, Balon's life was spared by the generous Robert.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Rawoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-5) Young Theon was given into the care of Lord Stark as a hostage and [ward](wiki:Ward) to ensure his defeated father's good behavior.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1)

### A Clash of Kings

[Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) recalls the fighting at [Pyke](wiki:Pyke) when he returns to the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) to treat with his father, [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy), [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-8) Balon declares independence again during the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) After his [capture of Winterfell](wiki:Capture_of_Winterfell), Theon defends his supposed killings of [Bran](wiki:Bran_Stark) and [Rickon Stark](wiki:Rickon_Stark) to his sister, [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy), claiming vengeance for [Rodrik](wiki:Rodrik_Greyjoy) and [Maron Greyjoy](wiki:Maron_Greyjoy).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

Ser [Jorah Mormont](wiki:Jorah_Mormont) tells [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) how he earned his [knighthood](wiki:Knight) at Pyke and met [Lynesse Hightower](wiki:Lynesse_Hightower).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5) [Arstan Whitebeard](wiki:Arstan_Whitebeard) later tells Jorah that he recalls having seen him at Pyke.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)

## Aftermath

Theon was taken away from Pyke on Robert's war galley and was raised alongside Eddard's children at [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7) Jorah was rewarded with [knighthood](wiki:Knight) by Robert for his bravery,(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) and Ser [Jacelyn Bywater](wiki:Jacelyn_Bywater) also received his knighthood at Pyke after losing his hand in the battle.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

Robert celebrated his victory by holding a [tourney at Lannisport](wiki:Tourney_at_Lannisport).(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) Jorah, victorious in the lists, won the hand of [Lynesse Hightower](wiki:Lynesse_Hightower) during the festivities.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Racok12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

Age and grief led Balon to become bitter.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) With Theon away on the mainland, Balon began to see his daughter, [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy), as his heir, treating her like he would a son.(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1)(wiki:Battle_at_Pyke.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)(wiki:Battle_at_
